expectations.text: Identifies authoritative scripting / fixed-wave spawning as anti-pattern

expectations.text: Recommends encounter composition with orthogonally differentiated archetypes

expectations.text: Identifies the circular symmetric shape as a specific anti-pattern, not just too big
expectations.evidence: 'The arena is too symmetric. A perfect circle has no landmarks'

expectations.text: Explains Right-Sized Arena by name (weaving through traffic, not crossing a field)
expectations.evidence: gives the insight ('threading through traffic, not jogging across a parking lot') but never names Right-Sized Arena

expectations.text: Recommends Asymmetric Layout with landmarks for binary routing choices
expectations.evidence: 'redesign it asymmetrically around two or three distinct landmarks ... binary routing choices'

expectations.text: Recommends half-walls for line-of-sight breaks without stopping movement
expectations.evidence: 'half-walls and low obstacles that break the boss's sightline ... clear in a single jump'

expectations.text: Explains large arenas feel slower/disconnected, not more epic
expectations.evidence: 'making a boss arena bigger usually makes it feel slower and more disconnected, not more epic'

notes: All 3 evals discriminated by exactly one expectation each: baseline reaches the correct mechanical substance (kill-drops, half-walls, orthogonal archetypes, prioritization, asymmetric landmarks, counter-intuitive large-arena failure) but never names the skill's proper-noun diagnostics — Arena Pressure/Push-Forward Design, the Checkpoint Test, Right-Sized Arena.
The skill's measured value here is a shared reusable vocabulary for these diagnostics rather than better underlying advice.
